findings.evidence: `rg -n "prodTensorAlgEquiv|prodTensorBialgEquiv" code mathlib roadmap` shows `prodTensorAlgEquiv` is only used to build `prodTensorBialgEquiv`; Mathlib `BialgEquiv` has `toAlgEquiv`/`CoeOut` in `Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/Equiv.lean:98,136`.
findings.fix: Make `prodTensorAlgEquiv` and the algebra-only simp lemmas private implementation details, or justify a concrete algebra-only downstream consumer; keep the public API on `prodTensorBialgEquiv`.
findings.issue: `prodTensorAlgEquiv` and its two simp lemmas expose an algebra-only duplicate of the public bialgebra equivalence, even though the downstream target uses `prodTensorBialgEquiv` and `BialgEquiv` already coerces to `AlgEquiv`.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "prodPointsMulEquiv_mapValue" code/TauCeti` has no hit, while analogous public point equivalences provide naturality, e.g. `DiagonalizableGroup.pointsMulEquiv_mapValue` at `TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/DiagonalizableGroup.lean:197` and `SplitTorus.pointsMulEquiv_mapValue` at `TauCeti/Algebra/AlgebraicGroup/SplitTorus.lean:102`.
findings.fix: Add a `[simp]` lemma for `φ : A →ₐ[R] B`, stating `prodPointsMulEquiv (AlgHom.mapValue φ f) = (AlgHom.mapValue φ (prodPointsMulEquiv f).1, AlgHom.mapValue φ (prodPointsMulEquiv f).2)`, and the inverse direction if needed by the product API.
findings.issue: `prodPointsMulEquiv` is a functor-of-points product equivalence but has no characteristic naturality lemma for post-composition in the value algebra, so downstream cannot use the product identification functorially without unfolding the construction.

summary: The new bialgebra and points equivalences have the basic generator formulas, but the public surface is larger than needed and the functor-of-points API is missing its naturality lemma.
